title: Co-Design, Equity, and Accessible Participation · Changed-Stakeholder Lab
summary: An independent public-benefit team supplies different users, constraints, risks, technology, data, and failure conditions and transfers co-design, equity, and accessible participation into a changed co-design plan and equity impact record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of co-design plan and equity impact record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.
Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.
Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Participation Level
steps.instruction:
lab case — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The lab variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Participation Level.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Participation Level, keep Participation Level connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The lab variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Participation Level condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Privacy Framework — Controls purpose, data processing, governance, communication, protection, retention, correction, and privacy-risk reasoning without presenting the draft as final.

At Commit to the mission model · Lab Participation Level, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Participation Level, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling a session co-design grants participants decision power.

options.text: State the Participation Level preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Calling a session co-design grants participants decision power. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
Append a pp2-feedback-accountability-alajdr: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feedback Accountability.

content.code:
pp2-feedback-accountability-alajdr:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Feedback Accountability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The lab variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
